feature: Mobile Polish - SafeArea, Back Button, Haptics, Build Settings
workflow_rationale: This is a feature task adding mobile-specific capabilities (SafeArea, back button handling, haptic feedback) across UI and game logic modules, plus Android build configuration changes. It follows service dependency: build settings first, then UI infrastructure (SafeArea), then input handling (back button), then game feedback (haptics), then integration verification.

phases.name: Android Build Settings & Orientation
phases.description: Configure ProjectSettings.asset for Android build: package name, API levels, portrait orientation lock, safe area rendering.

subtasks.description: Update ProjectSettings.asset: Set package name to com.ac247.brickgame, Android min API 26, target API 34, portrait orientation lock (defaultScreenOrientation: 1), disable autorotation to landscape, disable androidRenderOutsideSafeArea

verification.expected: defaultScreenOrientation: 1, Android: com.ac247.brickgame, AndroidMinSdkVersion: 26, AndroidTargetSdkVersion: 34, androidRenderOutsideSafeArea: 0

subtasks.notes: All 9 ProjectSettings.asset changes applied and verified: defaultScreenOrientation: 1 (Portrait), package name: com.ac247.brickgame, AndroidMinSdkVersion: 26, AndroidTargetSdkVersion: 34, disabled landscape autorotation, disabled useOSAutorotation, disabled androidRenderOutsideSafeArea. Committed as 1f96ae4.

phases.name: Safe Area Handler
phases.description: Create SafeAreaHandler MonoBehaviour component that adjusts a RectTransform to fit within the device safe area (notch/punch-hole avoidance).

subtasks.description: Create SafeAreaHandler.cs MonoBehaviour component. Attach to a RectTransform under Canvas. On Awake/orientation change, read Screen.safeArea and adjust RectTransform anchors to keep all child UI within the safe area. Follow existing UI component patterns.
subtasks.notes: Created SafeAreaHandler.cs MonoBehaviour component with: [RequireComponent(typeof(RectTransform))], Canvas parent caching, Screen.safeArea reading on Awake, anchor normalization (safeArea pixel coords / screen dimensions), Update() change detection via lastSafeArea/lastScreenSize/lastOrientation caching, Mathf.Clamp01 safety on anchor values. Verification passed: 4 occurrences of Screen.safeArea. Committed as 81d0292.

phases.name: Haptic Feedback System
phases.description: Create HapticManager utility and integrate haptic feedback on brick destruction. Respect Settings_Vibration PlayerPrefs toggle from 004-settings-screen.

subtasks.description: Create HapticManager.cs static utility class. Methods: Vibrate() using Handheld.Vibrate() wrapped in #if UNITY_ANDROID || UNITY_IOS, IsEnabled property reading PlayerPrefs 'Settings_Vibration' key (default: enabled), SetEnabled(bool). Follow SoundManager PlayerPrefs pattern.
subtasks.notes: HapticManager is a static class (POCO pattern, not MonoBehaviour). PlayerPrefs key: 'Settings_Vibration' (int, 1=on 0=off, default 1). For short vibrations on Android, use AndroidJavaObject to call android.os.Vibrator with a short duration (50ms). Fallback to Handheld.Vibrate() if native access fails. On iOS, use Handheld.Vibrate(). Wrap all platform calls in #if UNITY_ANDROID / #if UNITY_IOS.

subtasks.description: Integrate haptic feedback into Brick.cs: Call HapticManager.Vibrate() when a brick is destroyed, inside the 'if (wave <= 0)' block in HandleBallCollision() and in TakeDamage().
subtasks.notes: Add HapticManager.Vibrate() in TWO places: 1) HandleBallCollision() inside 'if (wave <= 0)' block just before AddScoreToPlayer, 2) TakeDamage() inside 'if (wave <= 0)' block just before AddScoreToPlayer. Only vibrate on destruction, not on every hit.

phases.name: Android Back Button Handling
phases.description: Handle Android back button (KeyCode.Escape) in each scene: StartUpScene shows exit confirmation, GameScene triggers pause, popup open → close popup.

subtasks.description: Add Android back button handling to UI_StartUpScene.cs: Add Update() method checking Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape). If popup open → close top popup. Otherwise → show exit confirmation (native Android dialog or toggle a confirmation panel, then Application.Quit on confirm).
subtasks.notes: Implementation: Add void Update() { if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape)) { HandleBackButton(); } }. HandleBackButton: 1) Check Managers.UI.GetPopupCount() > 0 → Managers.UI.ClosePopupUI() and return, 2) Otherwise show exit confirmation. For exit confirmation, simplest approach: use a bool flag _showExitConfirm, toggle visibility of a confirmation panel (Bind an exit confirm panel with Yes/No buttons), or use a simple approach where pressing back twice within 2 seconds quits. The existing ExitButton already calls Application.Quit(), so the exit logic is proven.

subtasks.description: Add Android back button handling to GameScene.cs: In existing Update() method, add KeyCode.Escape check at the top. If popup open → close it. If game playing → call PauseGame(). If game paused → ResumeGame().

verification.expected: KeyCode.Escape and PauseGame found in GameScene.cs

subtasks.notes: Add at TOP of existing Update() method, before the focus check: if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape)) { HandleBackButton(); return; }. HandleBackButton logic: 1) if (Managers.UI.GetPopupCount() > 0) { Managers.UI.ClosePopupUI(); return; }, 2) var brickGame = Managers.Game?.BrickGame; if (brickGame == null) return; 3) if (brickGame.Phase == GamePhase.Playing) { brickGame.PauseGame(); /* optionally show UI_PausePopup if it exists */ }, 4) else if (brickGame.Phase == GamePhase.Paused) { brickGame.ResumeGame(); }. BrickGameManager.PauseGame() and ResumeGame() already exist.

phases.name: Integration & Compile Verification
phases.description: Verify all components compile together, generate .meta files, check no missing references.

subtasks.description: Generate .meta files for new .cs files (SafeAreaHandler.cs.meta, HapticManager.cs.meta). Verify all cross-references: HapticManager used in Brick.cs, back button code in scene scripts. Final review of all changes.

verification.command: find Assets/@Scripts -name 'SafeAreaHandler*' -o -name 'HapticManager*' | sort && echo '---' && grep -rn 'HapticManager' Assets/@Scripts/Controllers/ && echo '---' && grep -rn 'KeyCode.Escape' Assets/@Scripts/
verification.expected: All files with .meta found, HapticManager referenced in Brick.cs, KeyCode.Escape in scene files

subtasks.notes: Unity .meta files need a unique GUID. Format: fileFormatVersion: 2, guid: <32char hex>, MonoImporter with serializedVersion 2. Generate random GUIDs for each .meta file. Also verify: 1) No namespace conflicts, 2) No circular dependencies, 3) All #if platform directives properly closed with #endif, 4) using statements are correct.

parallel_groups.reason: All 4 implementation phases have no dependencies on each other. They modify different files: phase-1=ProjectSettings.asset, phase-2=new SafeAreaHandler.cs, phase-3=new HapticManager.cs+Brick.cs, phase-4=UI_StartUpScene.cs+GameScene.cs. No file conflicts.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Single worker recommended - all phases are small and sequential execution is simpler for a single-service Unity project

acceptance_criteria: All new C# files compile without errors
SafeAreaHandler component adjusts RectTransform to Screen.safeArea
Android back button triggers pause in GameScene and exit confirm in StartUpScene
Haptic feedback fires on brick destruction (when enabled)

verification_steps.name: Compile Check
verification_steps.command: grep -rn 'class SafeAreaHandler' Assets/@Scripts/ && grep -rn 'class HapticManager' Assets/@Scripts/ && grep -rn 'HapticManager.Vibrate' Assets/@Scripts/Controllers/Object/BrickGame/Brick.cs

verification_steps.name: ProjectSettings Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Package name and API level correctly set

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk - creates new utility classes and modifies existing game logic (Brick.cs) and scene scripts. Changes are additive. ProjectSettings changes are straightforward YAML edits.

checks: Open project in Unity Editor - verify 0 compile errors
Check ProjectSettings > Player > Android > Package Name = com.ac247.brickgame
Check ProjectSettings > Player > Resolution > Default Orientation = Portrait
Play StartUpScene, press Escape - exit confirmation appears
Play GameScene, press Escape - game pauses
Play GameScene, destroy a brick - haptic triggers (test on device)
Build APK for Android - build succeeds
